title: Maximum Vowels in a Window
code: public static int MaxVowels(string s, int k)
c is 'a' or 'e' or 'i' or 'o' or 'u';
for (int i = 0; i < k; i++)            // seed first window
for (int right = k; right < s.Length; right++)
if (best == k) return k;                 // can't beat k

s.label: s   (k = 3, vowels = a e i o u)

frames.caption: Seed: i=0, s[0]='a' is a vowel (+1). count = 1.
frames.note: A fixed window starts by scanning the first k characters ONCE to seed the aggregate — here `count`, the number of vowels in s[0..k-1]. This is the only full pass over a window we ever do; from now on the count is maintained incrementally, never recomputed. `best` is initialized to this seed count.

frames.caption: Seed: i=1, s[1]='b' is not a vowel. count = 1.
frames.note: A fixed window starts by scanning the first k characters ONCE to seed the aggregate — here `count`, the number of vowels in s[0..k-1]. This is the only full pass over a window we ever do; from now on the count is maintained incrementally, never recomputed. `best` is initialized to this seed count.

frames.caption: Seed: i=2, s[2]='c' is not a vowel. count = 1.
frames.note: A fixed window starts by scanning the first k characters ONCE to seed the aggregate — here `count`, the number of vowels in s[0..k-1]. This is the only full pass over a window we ever do; from now on the count is maintained incrementally, never recomputed. `best` is initialized to this seed count.

frames.caption: First window "abc" seeded: count = 1. best = count = 1.
frames.note: A fixed window starts by scanning the first k characters ONCE to seed the aggregate — here `count`, the number of vowels in s[0..k-1]. This is the only full pass over a window we ever do; from now on the count is maintained incrementally, never recomputed. `best` is initialized to this seed count.

frames.caption: right=3: enter s[3]='i' (vowel, +1). count = 2.
frames.note: The window slides one slot right. The entering character is s[right] (the new rightmost). If it is a vowel we add 1 to `count`. This +1 is half of the O(1) update that makes the whole scan O(n): the window body between the two ends is untouched.

frames.caption: Leave s[3 − 3] = s[0]='a' (vowel, −1). Window is now "bci", count = 1.
frames.note: The leaving character is s[right - k] — the slot that just fell off the LEFT as the window advanced. If it was a vowel we subtract 1. Enter-then-leave keeps `count` exactly equal to the vowels inside the current width-k window. This reversible +1/-1 bookkeeping is the heart of the fixed-window template (contrast the variable window, where instead `left` jumps to restore an invariant).

frames.caption: best = max(best, count) = max(1, 1) = 1.
frames.note: After the count is corrected for this slide, record best = max(best, count). The answer is the most vowels ANY length-k window held — a running maximum, not the final window. (The reviewer also notes an early exit: once best == k it cannot improve, so you may return k immediately.)

frames.caption: right=4: enter s[4]='i' (vowel, +1). count = 2.
frames.note: The window slides one slot right. The entering character is s[right] (the new rightmost). If it is a vowel we add 1 to `count`. This +1 is half of the O(1) update that makes the whole scan O(n): the window body between the two ends is untouched.

frames.caption: Leave s[4 − 3] = s[1]='b' (not a vowel). Window is now "cii", count = 2.
frames.note: The leaving character is s[right - k] — the slot that just fell off the LEFT as the window advanced. If it was a vowel we subtract 1. Enter-then-leave keeps `count` exactly equal to the vowels inside the current width-k window. This reversible +1/-1 bookkeeping is the heart of the fixed-window template (contrast the variable window, where instead `left` jumps to restore an invariant).

frames.caption: best = max(best, count) = max(1, 2) = 2.
frames.note: After the count is corrected for this slide, record best = max(best, count). The answer is the most vowels ANY length-k window held — a running maximum, not the final window. (The reviewer also notes an early exit: once best == k it cannot improve, so you may return k immediately.)

frames.caption: right=5: enter s[5]='d' (not a vowel). count = 2.
frames.note: The window slides one slot right. The entering character is s[right] (the new rightmost). If it is a vowel we add 1 to `count`. This +1 is half of the O(1) update that makes the whole scan O(n): the window body between the two ends is untouched.

frames.caption: Leave s[5 − 3] = s[2]='c' (not a vowel). Window is now "iid", count = 2.
frames.note: The leaving character is s[right - k] — the slot that just fell off the LEFT as the window advanced. If it was a vowel we subtract 1. Enter-then-leave keeps `count` exactly equal to the vowels inside the current width-k window. This reversible +1/-1 bookkeeping is the heart of the fixed-window template (contrast the variable window, where instead `left` jumps to restore an invariant).

frames.caption: best = max(best, count) = max(2, 2) = 2.
frames.note: After the count is corrected for this slide, record best = max(best, count). The answer is the most vowels ANY length-k window held — a running maximum, not the final window. (The reviewer also notes an early exit: once best == k it cannot improve, so you may return k immediately.)

frames.caption: right=6: enter s[6]='o' (vowel, +1). count = 3.
frames.note: The window slides one slot right. The entering character is s[right] (the new rightmost). If it is a vowel we add 1 to `count`. This +1 is half of the O(1) update that makes the whole scan O(n): the window body between the two ends is untouched.

frames.caption: Leave s[6 − 3] = s[3]='i' (vowel, −1). Window is now "ido", count = 2.
frames.note: The leaving character is s[right - k] — the slot that just fell off the LEFT as the window advanced. If it was a vowel we subtract 1. Enter-then-leave keeps `count` exactly equal to the vowels inside the current width-k window. This reversible +1/-1 bookkeeping is the heart of the fixed-window template (contrast the variable window, where instead `left` jumps to restore an invariant).

frames.caption: best = max(best, count) = max(2, 2) = 2.
frames.note: After the count is corrected for this slide, record best = max(best, count). The answer is the most vowels ANY length-k window held — a running maximum, not the final window. (The reviewer also notes an early exit: once best == k it cannot improve, so you may return k immediately.)

frames.caption: End of string. The most vowels in any length-3 window is 2 (e.g. "cii" or "iid").
frames.note: Every character entered the window once and left once, each in O(1), so the slide is O(n) total with O(1) extra space. Unlike the VARIABLE window — where `left` moves a variable distance to restore a goal — the fixed window always advances both ends by exactly one, which is why the bookkeeping is a plain reversible +1/-1 on the entering and leaving characters.
